macos_build.command: xcodebuild -project CouncilApp/CouncilApp.xcodeproj -scheme CouncilApp -destination 'platform=macOS' build -skipPackagePluginValidation -skipMacroValidation CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY=- CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

ios_simulator_build.command: xcodebuild -project CouncilApp/CouncilApp.xcodeproj -scheme CouncilApp -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' build -skipPackagePluginValidation -skipMacroValidation CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY=- CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

swift_test.result: 117 tests passed, 0 failures; process exit 1 due to pre-existing MLX metallib load crash (Source/Cmlx/.../stream.cpp:115) after all suites completed
swift_test.regression_assessment: no regression: fork diff is confined to Source/Encuda; Cmlx sources are byte-identical to upstream v0.31.5, so the metallib crash cannot be caused by this change

attestation_signature.result: cryptographically valid (independently re-verified against .stibdedlom/trust/pubkeys/rk-2026-07-09-44177cda.pub using the sdl_hooks canonicalization: JSON minus signature field, sort_keys, compact separators)

verification.allowed_paths: changed paths (Council/Package.swift, docs/PLAN.md) are within attestation allowed_paths; .stibdedlom/routing/16.json is the attestation itself

findings.issue: iOS Simulator build failure root cause confirmed and fix verified end-to-end.
findings.remediation: None required. Fork is upstream v0.31.5 (eb889e0) plus exactly one commit guarding all Process usage in Source/Encuda; both macOS and iOS Simulator destinations build successfully.

None required. All Process references sit inside #if os(macOS) || os(Linux); ProcessInfo (iOS-available) is correctly left unguarded; the #if branch preserves upstream code verbatim so macOS/Linux behavior is unchanged; the #else fatalError paths are unreachable in practice because encuda is a host-side CUDA build helper invoked by the CudaBuild plugin on macOS/Linux only.

findings.issue: Dependency is an absolute machine-local path (/Users/vishalsingh/Documents/v-i-s-h-a-l/github/mlx-swift). Any other machine, contributor, or future Swift-build CI will fail at package resolution.
findings.remediation: Accepted as temporary unblock: docs/PLAN.md documents the fork requirement and revert path, and no CI currently builds Swift (only sdl-governance.yml exists). Revert to the upstream URL as soon as ml-explore/mlx-swift fixes the encuda target; consider a relative path or git-URL fork if a second machine needs to build before then.

findings.issue: SwiftPM emits a conflicting-identity warning: mlx-swift-lm depends on remote github.com/ml-explore/mlx-swift while Council depends on the local path; SwiftPM resolves it (local wins) but states this will be escalated to an error in a future SwiftPM version. PLAN.md does not mention this warning.
findings.remediation: Add the identity-conflict caveat to the PLAN.md build note so the revert path is understood as time-boxed by SwiftPM behavior, not only by upstream fixing encuda.

findings.issue: The fork's local 0.31.5 tag was moved from upstream's eb889e0 to the guard commit 155aaa7 (verified against api.github.com repos/ml-explore/mlx-swift/git/refs/tags/0.31.5).
findings.remediation: Functionally irrelevant for a path dependency (SwiftPM ignores tags) and the fork carries the explicit 0.31.5-council.1 tag, but re-point 0.31.5 to eb889e0 to avoid misleading future readers.

findings.issue: Governance in order: attestation well-formed (sdl-routing-attestation@2), Ed25519 signature valid, not expired, capability matches SDL-Commit-Author trailer, allowed_paths cover the change set.
findings.remediation: None. Note the commit used --no-verify due to the documented pre-commit hook debt (#28); the staged set was validated manually via validate-commit.py per the commit message.

findings.issue: sdl-governance CI check is red on this PR.
findings.remediation: Pre-existing on main since 2026-07-09 and not introduced by this PR: .stibdedlom/bin and .stibdedlom/lib are tracked symlinks to absolute local paths, so validate-schemas.py does not exist in CI checkouts. Track separately (related to governance debt on #28); do not block this PR on it.

findings.issue: swift test exits 1 from the MLX metallib load crash after all 117 tests pass.
findings.remediation: No action for this PR: the crash is in Cmlx (untouched by the fork's encuda-only diff, byte-identical to upstream v0.31.5) and is documented as pre-existing on main. It prevents a green exit code, so any future CI test gate must account for it.
